description: this course introduces you to cybersecurity for the cloud. we ll learn and apply classic security techniques to todays cloud security problems. we start with a deceptively simple and secure web service and address the problems arising as we improve it. well analyze recent cloud security vulnerabilities using standard systematic techniques. well build our own web service case studies and construct security solutions for them. our toolkit contains classic security concepts like least privilege and separation of duty as well as more technical cryptographic and access control techniques. here we introduce internet server security in six steps covering the first three in detail. we examine techniques to control how different hosts interact within a network. modern systems rarely reside inside a single trust boundary. we use cryptography to protect information when it travels outside our trust boundary. the public cloud provides both operational benefits and security challenges. we use concepts from the earlier modules to address cloud security challenges.
target_learner: This course is ideal for IT professionals, developers, or system administrators who are new to cloud environments and need a foundational understanding of cybersecurity principles applied to modern cloud infrastructure and web services. It suits individuals with basic technical literacy looking to specialize in security.
prerequisite_skills: Learners should possess a fundamental understanding of internet concepts, basic networking principles, and how web services generally function. No prior cybersecurity experience is required, but general IT familiarity will be beneficial.
skills_gained: Upon completion, learners will be able to analyze cloud security vulnerabilities, design and implement security solutions for web services, and apply classic security concepts like least privilege and separation of duty. They will also gain practical skills in using cryptographic and access control techniques for data protection and network interaction control.
learning_outcomes: Analyze recent cloud security vulnerabilities using systematic techniques. Construct and apply security solutions for web service case studies, integrating principles like least privilege and separation of duty. Utilize cryptographic and access control methods to secure information and manage host interactions within cloud networks.
career_relevance: This course provides a critical foundation for roles such as Junior Cloud Security Analyst, Security-focused Web Developer, or System Administrator transitioning to cloud operations. It is relevant across all industries leveraging public cloud platforms and serves as an entry point into specialized cloud security careers.
next_steps: Learners should consider exploring more advanced courses focused on specific cloud provider security (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP Security), network security, or ethical hacking. Pursuing certifications like CompTIA Security+ or entry-level cloud security certifications would also be a logical progression.

description: machine learning ml allows us to teach computers to make predictions and decisions based on data and learn from experiences. in recent years incredible optimizations have been made to machine learning algorithms software frameworks and embedded hardware. thanks to this running deep neural networks and other complex machine learning algorithms is possible on low power devices like microcontrollers. this course will give you a broad overview of how machine learning works how to train neural networks and how to deploy those networks to microcontrollers which is known as embedded machine learning or tinyml. you do not need any prior machine learning knowledge to take this course. familiarity with arduino and microcontrollers is advised to understand some topics as well as to tackle the projects. some math reading plots arithmetic algebra is also required for quizzes and projects. we will cover the concepts and vocabulary necessary to understand the fundamentals of machine learning as well as provide demonstrations and projects to give you hands on experience. in this module we will introduce the concept of machine learning how it can be used to solve problems and its limitations. we will also cover how machine learning on embedded systems such as single board computers and microcontrollers can be effectively used to solve problems and create new types of computer interfaces. then we will introduce the edge impulse tool and collect motion data for a magic wand demo. finally we will examine the various features that can be calculated from this raw motion data including root mean square rms fourier transform and power spectral density psd in this module we will look at how neural networks work how to train them and how to use them to perform inference in an embedded system. we will continue the previous demo of creating a motion classification system using motion data collected from a smartphone or arduino board. finally we will challenge you with a new motion classification project where you will have the opportunity to implement the concepts learning in this module and the previous module. in this module we cover audio classification on embedded systems. specifically we will go over the basics of extracting mel frequency cepstral coefficients mfccs as features from recorded audio training a convolutional neural network cnn and deploying that neural network to a microcontroller. additionally we dive into some of the implementation strategies on embedded systems and talk about how machine learning compares to sensor fusion.
target_learner: This course is ideal for embedded systems developers, IoT engineers, and hardware enthusiasts who want to integrate machine learning into low-power devices. It suits individuals with an interest in bridging hardware and AI, even without prior machine learning experience.
prerequisite_skills: Learners should have familiarity with microcontrollers, such as Arduino, to understand project contexts and hardware interactions. Basic mathematical skills, including arithmetic, algebra, and the ability to interpret plots, are necessary for quizzes and practical exercises.
skills_gained: Upon completion, learners will be proficient in fundamental machine learning concepts, neural network training, and deployment on microcontrollers (TinyML). They will gain hands-on experience with data collection, feature extraction (e.g., RMS, Fourier Transform, MFCCs), and implementing motion and audio classification systems using the Edge Impulse platform.
learning_outcomes: Articulate the core principles of machine learning and its specific applications within embedded systems. Design, train, and deploy neural networks for practical motion and audio classification tasks on microcontrollers. Effectively extract and process sensor data features to prepare datasets for embedded machine learning models.
career_relevance: This course directly supports career advancement in the rapidly growing fields of IoT, edge computing, and embedded AI. It prepares individuals for specialized roles such as TinyML Engineer, Embedded AI Developer, or Smart Device Architect, enabling them to build intelligent, low-power solutions across various industries.
next_steps: Consider exploring advanced deep learning architectures optimized for resource-constrained environments or diving deeper into specific embedded platforms. Pursue projects involving different sensor modalities (e.g., vision, environmental data) with TinyML, or investigate MLOps practices tailored for embedded deployments.

course_title: microsoft azure machine learning for data scientists
description: machine learning is at the core of artificial intelligence and many modern applications and services depend on predictive machine learning models. training a machine learning model is an iterative process that requires time and compute resources. automated machine learning can help make it easier. in this course you will learn how to use azure machine learning to create and publish models without writing code. this is the second course in a five course program that prepares you to take the dp 100 designing and implementing a data science solution on azurecertification exam. the certification exam is an opportunity to prove knowledge and expertise operate machine learning solutions at a cloud scale using azure machine learning. this specialization teaches you to leverage your existing knowledge of python and machine learning to manage data ingestion and preparation model training and deployment and machine learning solution monitoring in microsoft azure. each course teaches you the concepts and skills that are measured by the exam. this specialization is intended for data scientists with existing knowledge of python and machine learning frameworks like scikit learn pytorch and tensorflow who want to build and operate machine learning solutions in the cloud. it teaches data scientists how to create end to end solutions in microsoft azure. students will learn how to manage azure resources for machine learning run experiments and train models deploy and operationalize machine learning solutions and implement responsible machine learning. they will also learn to use azure databricks to explore prepare and model data and integrate databricks machine learning processes with azure machine learning. training a machine learning model is an iterative process that requires time and compute resources. automated machine learning can help make it easier. in this module you ll learn how to identify different kinds of machine learning model and how to use the automated machine learning capability of azure machine learning to train and deploy a predictive model. regression is a supervised machine learning technique used to predict numeric values. in this module you will learn how to create regression models using azure machine learning designer. classification is a supervised machine learning technique used to predict categories or classes. in this module you will learn how to create classification models using azure machine learning designer. clustering is an unsupervised machine learning technique used to group similar entities based on their features. in this module you will learn how to create clustering models using azure machine learning designer.
target_learner: This course is ideal for experienced data scientists who possess a strong background in Python and familiarity with machine learning frameworks like scikit-learn, PyTorch, or TensorFlow. It targets those looking to transition their ML model development and operationalization skills to the Microsoft Azure cloud platform, particularly using automated tools.
prerequisite_skills: Learners should have solid proficiency in Python programming and a foundational understanding of core machine learning concepts and algorithms. Prior experience with common ML libraries and a basic grasp of data science workflows are essential for success.
skills_gained: Upon completion, learners will be able to leverage Azure Machine Learning for end-to-end solution development, including managing cloud resources for ML. They will gain expertise in implementing automated machine learning (AutoML) and using Azure ML Designer to build and deploy regression, classification, and clustering models. The course also covers integrating Azure Databricks for data preparation and implementing responsible ML practices.
learning_outcomes: Learners will be able to design and implement automated machine learning pipelines within Azure ML to efficiently train and deploy predictive models. They will also be capable of developing and operationalizing various supervised and unsupervised machine learning models using the Azure ML Designer interface. Finally, learners will effectively manage Azure cloud resources for machine learning experiments and solution deployment.
career_relevance: This course directly supports data scientists and ML engineers aiming to specialize in cloud-based machine learning solutions on Microsoft Azure. It is highly relevant for roles focused on building, deploying, and monitoring scalable AI applications, and is a key step towards achieving the Microsoft DP-100 certification, validating expertise in Azure ML.
next_steps: To fully prepare for the DP-100 certification, learners should proceed with the remaining three courses in the "Designing and Implementing a Data Science Solution on Azure" specialization. Further learning could involve exploring advanced MLOps practices within Azure or delving deeper into specific deep learning frameworks integrated with Azure ML.

course_title: probability and statistics to p or not to p
description: we live in an uncertain and complex world yet we continually have to make decisions in the present with uncertain future outcomes. indeed we should be on the look out for black swans low probability high impact events. to study or not to study to invest or not to invest to marry or not to marry while uncertainty makes decision making difficult it does at least make life exciting if the entire future was known in advance there would never be an element of surprise. whether a good future or a bad future it would be a known future. in this course we consider many useful tools to deal with uncertainty and help us to make informed and hence better decisions essential skills for a lifetime of good decision making. key topics include quantifying uncertainty with probability descriptive statistics point and interval estimation of means and proportions the basics of hypothesis testing and a selection of multivariate applications of key terms and concepts seen throughout the course.
target_learner: Individuals seeking to improve their decision-making skills in uncertain environments, including students new to quantitative methods and professionals needing foundational data literacy. It is ideal for anyone who regularly encounters data and needs to make informed choices.
prerequisite_skills: A basic understanding of arithmetic and elementary algebra is beneficial for grasping the mathematical concepts. No prior statistics or advanced mathematical knowledge is required, making it accessible for beginners.
skills_gained: Quantifying uncertainty using probability, applying descriptive statistics, performing point and interval estimation of means and proportions, conducting basic hypothesis tests, and interpreting multivariate applications of statistical concepts.
learning_outcomes: Apply fundamental probability concepts to assess and quantify uncertainty in various real-world situations. Utilize descriptive statistics and estimation methods to analyze data and infer population characteristics. Formulate and test basic hypotheses to support data-driven decision-making processes.
career_relevance: Essential for roles in business analysis, finance, marketing, public policy, and research where data interpretation, risk assessment, and informed decision-making are critical. It supports entry-level data analysis, strategic planning, and project management positions across various industries.
next_steps: Consider follow-on courses in advanced inferential statistics, regression analysis, or data science with programming languages like R or Python. Explore specialized applications in fields such as financial modeling, epidemiological statistics, or experimental design.

organizations financial position. applied learning project the financial reporting specialization includes multiple projects and practice activities including analyses of statements and cases which will be peer reviewed. in addition the specialization includes one capstone course which is comprised of a full case analysis of an actual companys financial statements. analyze the structure and its role in generating financial statements critically examine key financial statements and assess their significance for external decision makers make informed decisions and assessments regarding financial statements and the overall financial health of an organization measure and report issues related to inventories long lived assets and pp e identify and account for debt and equity investments determine the appropriate classification measure and disclosure of major asset categories describe the accounting process related to accounting for liabilities from origination of transactions to disclosure in the financial statements describe liabilities and contingencies and demonstrate the appropriate accounting treatment including required disclosures demonstrate the accounting for bonds including par discount and premium bonds explain and demonstrate the accounting for pension plans explain and demonstrate the financial accounting for income taxes describe the target_learner: This intermediate specialization is ideal for aspiring accountants, financial analysts, and auditors seeking to master the intricacies of financial reporting. It also suits business professionals with foundational accounting knowledge aiming to deepen their expertise in statement preparation and analysis.
prerequisite_skills: Learners should possess a fundamental understanding of accounting principles, including the accounting equation, debits and credits, and the basic structure and purpose of the three primary financial statements. Familiarity with general business operations is also beneficial.
skills_gained: Financial statement preparation and analysis, application of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), accounting for complex assets and liabilities, financial health assessment, earnings per share calculation, and critical evaluation of financial disclosures.
learning_outcomes: Apply advanced accounting principles to accurately prepare and process an organization's comprehensive financial statements. Critically analyze financial statements to evaluate an organization's financial position, performance, and cash flows for external decision-making. Account for complex transactions related to assets, liabilities, equity, and income taxes, ensuring proper measurement and disclosure.
career_relevance: This specialization directly supports career paths in public accounting, corporate finance, auditing, and financial analysis. It equips professionals to excel in roles such as Financial Accountant, Reporting Analyst, Auditor, or Controller, where a deep understanding of GAAP and financial statement integrity is paramount.
next_steps: Consider pursuing professional accounting certifications like the CPA or CMA to validate expertise. Further specialization in areas such as forensic accounting, advanced taxation, or financial modeling would build upon this strong foundation. An MBA with a finance concentration or a Master's in Accounting could also be logical next steps.

description: the two key components of any data pipeline are data lakes and warehouses. this course highlights use cases for each type of storage and dives into the available data lake and warehouse solutions on google cloud in technical detail. also this course describes the role of a data engineer the benefits of a successful data pipeline to business operations and examines why data engineering should be done in a cloud environment. this is the first course of the data engineering on google cloud series. after completing this course enroll in the building batch data pipelines on google cloud course. this module introduces the data engineering on google cloud source series and this modernizing data lakes and data warehouses with google cloud course. this module discusses the role of data engineering and motivates the claim why data engineering should be done in the cloud 1 app item in this module we describe what data lake is and how to use cloud storage as your data lake on google cloud. 1 app item in this module we talk about bigquery as a data warehousing option on google cloud 3 app items a summary of the key learning points

description: this is primarily aimed at first and second year undergraduates interested in psychology statistics data analysis and research methods along with high school students and professionals with similar interests. this module introduces you to your psyclearn essentials course. find out whats included in this course and how to navigate the modules and lessons. youll also learn valuable study tips for successful learning. this course will begin by introducing the basic concepts of how to describe and visualize data the fundamentals of using statistics to make inferences and the logic of null hypothesis testing. various types of hypothesis tests will be introduced along with criteria for selecting which is appropriate for different study conditions. as an extension of null hypothesis significance tests you will learn about how to interpret effect sizes and confidence intervals along with statistical power before being introduced to alternatives to null hypothesis significance testing. in this first section of the course the fundamental concept of the variable is introduced and explained along with some of the basic statistical methods we use to describe and summarize variables in data sets. in this module we move on from descriptive statistics like the mean and standard deviation into inferential statistics which help us use sample data to draw inferences about the populations the samples represent. in this section of the course we consider the role of null hypothesis significance testing in psychological research some objections that have apas online tool for writing effectively as well as valuable advice that will help you develop and strengthen your skillset for learning success and future employment. additionally explore resources skills_gained: Learners will gain proficiency in describing and visualizing data, applying descriptive statistics, and utilizing inferential statistics to draw conclusions from samples. description: plastic electronics is a concept that emerged forty years ago with the discovery of electrically conductive polymers. ten years later the first electronic devices using organic solids in place of the ubiquitous inorganic semiconductors were realised. the best achievement of plastic electronics is constituted by organic light emitting diodes oleds that equip the display of many smartphones and even tv sets. the objective of this course is to provide a comprehensive overview of the physics of plastic electronic devices. after taking this course the student should be able to demonstrate theoretical knowledge on the following subjects concept of organic semiconductors charge carrier transport in polymeric and organic semiconductors optical properties of organic semiconductors charge injection from metals to organic solids operating mode of the main plastic electronic devices organic light emitting diodes oleds organic photovoltaic cells opvs and organic field effect transistors ofets this first module is an introduction to the concept of semiconductor which is the primary consituent of all electronic decives. we also show how some organic compounds can behave as semiconductors. electronic devives work through an electrical current generated by applying an electrical voltage. the process is controlled by charge carrier transport descibed in this module. beside electrical properties electronic devices also involve optical aspects for example in light emitting diodes and photovoltaic cells. this modulel describes the interactions of light with organic semiconductors.

description: this specialization is for those who want to study english grammar at the beginning level. through the three courses in this specialization you will learn the fundamental features of english grammar such as word forms verb tenses and question and answer formation so that you can start your english learning journey on the right path. applied learning project in each course learners will use the grammar patterns they learn after every lesson to complete practice exercises and quizzes. learners will complete activities to practice using english not only at the sentence level but also at the discourse level. in this course word forms and simple present tense you will learn about different word forms like nouns proper nouns plural forms and singular forms. you will learn when to use the articles a and an. you will also learn about the be verb in english how to form it and when to use it. then you will learn how to form other verbs in the simple present including some spelling rules and how to make negative forms. in this course questions present progressive and future tenses you will learn about question words in english and how to form questions in the simple present. then you will learn how to form the present progressive to express ideas about things that are happening right now. you will look at the differences between the simple present and present progressive. finally you will learn how to use to be going to and the present progressive to talk about the future and how to use can to talk about ability. in this course simple past tense you will learn how to form and use the simple past. you will see that there are a lot of irregular verbs in the simple past but there are some patterns you can use to help you remember. you will learn about spelling changes when you add an ed ending as well as how to form negatives and questions in the past. you will also learn about object pronouns and some other forms of adjectives to describe and make comparisons.

course_title: teach english now lesson design and assessment
description: learners will be introduced to designing lesson plans based on principles and knowledge of learning objectives assessment plans methods materials and learning activities. learners will find and prepare appropriate teaching materials through careful analysis adaptation and creation of professional resources. learners will also reflect on the cohesion between lesson design and teaching philosophies. in this module learners are introduced to lesson design based on theorists such as robert gagne and benjamin bloom. learners are introduced to concepts to both begin a lesson gain attention state objectives stimulate recall and give a lesson present the content provide learning guidance elicit performance this module also introduces learners to a general lesson plan template that will be used throughout the course and to basic classroom management skills. in this module learners are introduced to warm up activities and how warm ups not only can introduce a lesson s theme but also can be used to connect that theme to previous learner knowledge and previous lessons. objective discussions are also introduced and advice about when and how to give objective discussions is given. learners are introduced to the concepts of presenting instruction and modeling. learners are introduced to the concept of stickiness and given several words of advice on how to make content sticky. modeling is also depicted as a way in which to help learners to see what teachers are trying to explain and can be a powerful form of instruction itself. learners are counseled to have more than one model and are cautioned about presenting models too early. in this module learners are introduced to the importance of guided and independent practices of lesson planning. in our three teachers videos learners will view different examples both strong and weak of teachers facilitate guided and independent practices and receive breakdowns and analysis of each teacher. in this module learners review the concept of clear objectives and are introduced to the idea of alignment. learners are introduced to the ideas of memory and knowledge learning and how to appropriately assess each type. learners are given several tips to make their lives easier regarding assessment learners see three teachers give assessments in their classrooms and hear analysis of each one. each of the concepts covered in modules 1 5 is summarized. learners will submit their completed lesson plan for review.

course_title: the science of the solar system
description: learn about the science behind the current exploration of the solar system in this free class. use principles from physics chemistry biology and geology to understand the latest from mars comprehend the outer solar system ponder planets outside our solar system and search for habitability in our neighborhood and beyond. this course is generally taught at an advanced level assuming a prior knowledge of undergraduate math and physics but the majority of the concepts and lectures can be understood without these prerequisites. the quizzes and final exam are designed to make you think critically about the material you have learned rather than to simply make you memorize facts. the class is expected to be challenging but rewarding.
target_learner: This course is ideal for intellectually curious individuals with a strong interest in space exploration and planetary science. It suits aspiring scientists, engineers, or educators who enjoy interdisciplinary challenges, even if they have a general science background rather than advanced undergraduate math and physics.
prerequisite_skills: A foundational understanding of high school level physics, chemistry, biology, and mathematics (algebra, basic calculus concepts) is beneficial. Strong critical thinking and analytical skills are essential for engaging with the material and succeeding in the assessment components.
skills_gained: Learners will acquire interdisciplinary scientific analysis skills, applying principles from physics, chemistry, biology, and geology to complex problems. They will develop critical thinking for interpreting scientific data from space missions and enhance their understanding of planetary science concepts.
learning_outcomes: Upon completion, learners will be able to analyze current and historical data from solar system missions, such as Mars exploration, using interdisciplinary scientific principles. They will also be able to evaluate the potential for habitability on various celestial bodies within and beyond our solar system.
career_relevance: This course is highly relevant for roles such as Planetary Scientist, Astrobiologist, Aerospace Engineer (research-focused), or Science Educator. It provides a strong academic foundation for careers within space agencies, aerospace companies, research institutions, and universities, supporting advancement in space exploration and scientific research.
next_steps: Consider pursuing advanced courses in Astrobiology, Planetary Geophysics, or Astrophysics to specialize further. Learners could also seek research opportunities or internships at space agencies or university laboratories to apply their newfound knowledge in practical settings.
